content:
- Read current bug task `.ralph/tasks/bugs/fencing-cutoff-commit-timestamp-zero-fallback-undercounts.md`; confirmed original issue in `src/ha/e2e_multi_node.rs` is already fixed (no sentinel `0` timestamps; fail-closed cutoff helper + unit tests).
- Ran repo audit for remaining sentinel time fallbacks; found `Err(_) => 0` in HA e2e timeline record helpers and harness cleanup, plus time-based tmp-path helpers in `src/test_harness/binaries.rs` tests.
- Removed all remaining `Err(_) => 0` / `unwrap_or(0)` time fallbacks in `src/ha` + `src/test_harness`:
  - record() functions now emit `time_error:{err}` instead of `[0]`.
  - port unlease cleanup no longer substitutes `0` on time failure.
  - binaries tests now use pid + atomic counter for unique tmp paths (no time dependency).
- Ran full required gates and saved logs under `.ralph/evidence/fencing-cutoff-commit-timestamp-zero-fallback-undercounts/`:
  - `make check`, `make test`, `make test-long`, `make lint` all green.
- Ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh` to switch off the task.

- Read .ralph/current_tasks.md and checked for failing tasks.
- Found one bug task with <passes>false</passes>: .ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-bdd-http-tests-false-pass-via-fragile-status-and-read-patterns.md.
- Wrote that path to .ralph/current_task.txt per bug-first rule.
- Next: execute the selected bug task (strengthen BDD HTTP contract tests to avoid false passes/hangs).

- Searched codebase for HTTP response parsing/read helpers relevant to BDD HTTP tests.
- Found `tests/bdd_api_http.rs` uses local `extract_status_and_body` + `read_to_end` and status `contains("xyz")` assertions, which are fragile.
- Found strongest existing parser utility pattern in `src/test_harness/ha_e2e/util.rs::parse_raw_http_response` (returns numeric status + body slice), plus robust request framing logic in `src/api/worker.rs::read_http_request` and `src/cli/client.rs` test helpers.
- Recommendation: reuse/adapt `parse_raw_http_response` style into a shared test helper for bdd_api_http, and add bounded response reader based on header/content-length instead of connection-close `read_to_end`.

- Read `.ralph/current_task.txt` -> `.ralph/tasks/bugs/bug-bdd-http-tests-false-pass-via-fragile-status-and-read-patterns.md`.
- Read task file and identified required work: harden BDD HTTP tests by replacing `status_line.contains(...)` and `read_to_end` patterns.
- Spawned explorer subagents to inspect `tests/bdd_api_http.rs`, find existing HTTP parsing helpers, and confirm server response semantics (`Content-Length` + `Connection: close`).
- Wrote a detailed implementation/verification plan into the task file, ending with `TO BE VERIFIED` as requested.
- Next: do deep skeptical verification of plan, change at least one aspect, then replace terminator with `NOW EXECUTE` before implementing.

- Inspected tests/bdd_api_http.rs end-to-end: local helper extract_status_and_body does string split + CRLF boundary scan; all tests use read_to_end and status_line.contains("XYZ") assertions.
- Cross-checked repo for httparse usage: request parsing in src/api/worker.rs uses httparse::Request with explicit Partial handling and fixed header array (64).
- Identified pitfalls to report: read_to_end requires server-side close/EOF, substring status asserts are fragile, helper copies body Vec and does not parse numeric status or headers.
- Prepared safer helper shape proposal: typed HttpTestResponse { status_code, headers, body }, send_request helper with timeout + Connection: close + httparse response parsing + explicit Result errors (no unwrap/expect).

- Investigated Node API response framing in src/api/worker.rs.
- Confirmed write_http_response always writes HTTP/1.1 status line + Content-Type + Content-Length + Connection: close, then body bytes; no Transfer-Encoding path exists.
- Confirmed step_once accepts one connection, reads one request, writes one response, returns; no per-connection keep-alive loop.
- Checked request parsing: only Content-Length body framing supported on requests; no chunked request decoding.
- Reviewed test helpers in src/api/worker.rs tests and tests/bdd_api_http.rs: currently rely on read_to_end and string contains on status lines, which couple tests to close-delimited reads and are weaker than numeric/header parsing.
- Next: provide user with strict/future-proof assertion strategy: central response parser validates header/body framing and optionally enforces current close contract in dedicated contract tests.

- User asked for codebase scan for HTTP parsing helpers/test utilities and httparse usage.
- Read progress log, then searched src/tests for parsing helpers and raw HTTP reads.
- Found duplicated `extract_status_and_body` in tests/bdd_api_http.rs and src/api/worker.rs test module; both parse status line as string + split on CRLFCRLF and callers assert `status_line.contains("...")`.
- Found separate `parse_raw_http_response` in src/test_harness/ha_e2e/util.rs that already parses numeric status code + body slice.
- Found `httparse` dependency is used in src/api/worker.rs request parsing only (read_http_request + parse_content_length), not in bdd/tests harness response helpers.
- Visibility constraint: src/test_harness is `pub(crate)` and cfg-gated, so integration tests under tests/ cannot directly consume helper there.
- Recommendation prepared: factor a shared HTTP test response helper into a new crate-level test-support module (public, test-focused) that both integration tests and in-crate tests can call; alternatively test_harness helper works only for crate-internal tests.

- User asked to test publish script and confirm auto-push behavior.
- First test run built mdBook but failed in sandbox at git remote set-url in ../pgtuskmaster-docs (.git/config permission denied).
- Elevated runs initially failed because scripts/publish-docs.sh had disappeared unexpectedly; verified scripts dir existed but file missing.
- Recreated scripts/publish-docs.sh with same mdBook->dist/out logic; chmod +x and bash -n passed.
- Ran script with escalated permissions end-to-end; success.
- mdBook build completed (with expected mdbook-mermaid version warning), docs synced into ../pgtuskmaster-docs/dist/out, commit created in docs repo (fbfb4749), and push to origin/main succeeded.
- Confirmed: script auto-pushes by default after staging/commit step.

- User requested behavior fix: if docs source/output unchanged, do not auto-update/push.
- Updated scripts/publish-docs.sh to exit early when docs target has no staged diff: message now "No new changes to commit; skipping push." and exit 0 before push.
- Kept push path for changed docs only.
- Ran bash -n validation.
- Committed only scripts/publish-docs.sh in this repo with commit 7941e14 (docs: skip publish push when no changes). No other modified files were staged.

- User requested auto-run of docs publish script on every commit after now.
- Implemented versioned git hook at .githooks/post-commit that runs scripts/publish-docs.sh after each commit and never blocks commit success.
- Configured repository git setting: core.hooksPath=.githooks.
- Committed only .githooks/post-commit as commit 37bd7a2.
- During this commit, hook executed and publish failed in sandbox due permission to ../pgtuskmaster-docs .git/config, but commit still succeeded by design (post-commit warning only).
